topics.introduction: This topic will look at demonstrating understanding of addition of whole numbers. Adding single digit numbers up to 9 using addition as an increase, as putting together and as comparison. Adding numbers using composition and decomposition of numbers (number bonds), Adding single digit numbers with a sum up to 18 (with carrying using the concept of ten as a unit), Adding whole numbers with sums up to 100 without carrying, completing addition of number sentences, using addition to carry out activities involving money and other quantities.

key_terms.definition: this is a fundamental operation that combines two or more numbers together to get a total or a sum.
a digit is a single symbol or character used to represent a value in a numerical system e.g. 1,2,3,4,5…………..
this refers to the process of combining two or more functions, relations, or operation to form a new number e.g., 2 plus 3 equals 5.
this is the breaking down of a complex problem, object, or system into smaller, or manageable parts or components.

this is a simple math concept that represents the relationship between three numbers, where two numbers are added together to equal a third number. For example, 2 plus 3 equals 5 (The bond number is 2,3 and 5)

sub_topics.title: Addition
sub_topics.introduction: This sub-topic focuses on demonstrating an understanding of addition of whole numbers.
competences: Demonstrate understanding of addition of whole numbers

teacher_steps: Ask learners to count from 1-9 individually
Guide learners to practically add numbers using counters e.g. 1 plus 1 equal to 2, 1 plus 2 is equal to 3 and 2 plus 2 is equal to 4 etc.
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Demonstrating understanding of addition of whole numbers by adding single digit numbers up to 9 using addition as an increase, as putting together and as comparison.

teacher_steps: Ask learners to add more complex numbers. For example, 2 plus 2 is equal to 4, then 3 plus 4 is equal to 8, and 4 plus 5 is equal to 9.
Demonstrate addition of numbers more than 2. For example, 3 plus 2 plus 1.
Ask learners to solve the problem (1 plus 4 plus 2)
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Adding numbers using composition and decomposition of numbers (number bonds)

teacher_steps: Explain to the learners the concept of recarrying. E.g., 9 plus 7 is equal to 16. Note: the act of carrying over a number to the next place value when adding two numbers together, where the sum in the ones is greater than 9
Demonstrate on how to add numbers by recarrying
Ask individual learners to demonstrate addition of recarrying (6 plus 7, 9 plus 5…….).
Put learners in pairs and assign them to solve the given problems by recarrying.
learner_steps: Observe demonstration of addition with recarrying
Solve problems by recarrying in pairs
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Adding single digit numbers with a sum up to 18 (with carrying using the concept of ten as a unit)

teacher_steps: Explain to the learners the concept that does not involve recarrying. E.g. 43 plus 24 in vertical arrangement.
Demonstrate on how to add numbers without recarrying
Ask individual learners to demonstrate addition without recarrying (22 plus 23, 31 plus 15…….).
Put learners in pairs and assign them to solve the given problems without recarrying.
learner_steps: Solve problems without recarrying in pairs
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Adding whole numbers with sums up to 100 without carrying.

teacher_steps: Demonstrate addition to complete the addition of number sentences.
Ask individual learners to complete a given a number sentence by adding the numbers correctly.
Put the learners into groups of fives and then assign them to work out given tasks in their respective groups.
Ask learners from each respective groups to the front to present and the teacher to consolidate
learner_steps: Work in groups to complete addition tasks

instructional_blocks.hook: What do we use to buy jiggies, sweets or biscuits?
teacher_steps: Assess the learners' knowledge about money through a question (what do we use to buy jiggies, sweets or biscuits?)

Display different notes and coins of money on the table for the learners to identify and relate the value of different denominations of money to their buying power. E.g., K2 can buy a packet of jiggies, whereas K1 can buy a sweet.
Ask learners to demonstrate addition involving money through a role play
Assign learners to individually add money using number sentences. For example; K10 + K5= K15.
learner_steps: Relate money values to buying power
Demonstrate addition involving money through role play
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Using addition to carry out activities involving money and other quantities.

questions: Teacher to use an audio-visual aid depicting the selling and buying of goods to assess the learners' awareness and knowledge of money and its value.

sub_topics.summary: This topic looked at demonstrating understanding of addition of whole numbers, adding single digit numbers up to 9 using addition as an increase, as putting together and as comparison, adding numbers using composition and decomposition of numbers (number bonds), adding single digit numbers with a sum up to 18 (with carrying using the concept of ten as a unit), adding whole numbers with sums up to 100 without carrying. It also looked on the aspect of completing addition of number sentences, using addition to carry out activities involving money and other quantities

topics.introduction: This topic focuses on exploring natural resources, comparing features of the environment, demonstrating an awareness of risks associated with household chemicals, developing care for the environment, developing awareness of weather occurrence, demonstrating awareness effects of climate change.

key_terms.definition: the immediate surrounding or condition where people or animals live or operate. This may include; family, friends, natural resources and the built environment
the condition of the day at a particular time and place
a long-term warming of the planet resulting in increase in average temperature caused by negative human activities.
any substance that can cause harm to a human being if not properly used
these are characteristics or components that define the product or a system.

sub_topics.title: Natural Resources
sub_topics.introduction: This sub-topic looks at exploring natural resources such as rivers, lakes, plants, animals, ant-hills, mountains and soil.

teacher_steps: Explain to the learners what natural resources are.
Take the learners outside to identify natural resources observed in the immediate environment.
Display visual/tactile teaching and learning aids for the learners to observe.
Ask learners to relate what was observed in the teaching and learning aids to what is found in their environment.
learner_steps: Identify natural resources in the immediate environment
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Exploring natural resources such as rivers, lakes, plants, animals, ant-hills, mountains and soil.

sub_topics.title: Natural and Man-made Environment
sub_topics.introduction: This sub-topic focuses on comparing features of the environment. Natural environment refers to some resources that are not man-made such as trees, rivers, sources that are not man-made.

teacher_steps: Explain to the learners what an urban environment is. Characteristics: Paved or tared roads, Piped water, Modern buildings, Modern markets, Motorized transport, Electricity
Explain sub-urban: a semi-developed environment lying between urban and rural. Characteristics: Graded/gravel roads, Piped water, Wells, Modern/traditional buildings
Explain rural: areas far from major towns. Characteristics: Traditional buildings, Well, stream and river source of water, Ungraded roads, Traditional modes of transport, Traditional lighting systems
Explain natural environment: environment that has its natural habitant. Characteristics: Forest, Natural water bodies, World animals, No roads, No buildings
Divide learners into groups to discuss, compare and contrast urban, suburban, rural, and natural environments.
learner_steps: Learn about urban, suburban, rural, and natural environments
Discuss characteristics of each environment type
Compare and contrast different environments in groups
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Comparing features such as urban, suburban, rural, or natural environments.

sub_topics.title: Household Chemicals
sub_topics.introduction: Household chemicals are chemicals found in a home. These may either be useful.
competences: Demonstrate an awareness of risks associated with household chemicals

teacher_steps: Explain what household chemicals are.
Display some household chemicals found in the home on the table for the learners to observe, name and tell their uses.
Consolidate the answers given by the learners
learner_steps: Name household chemicals and their uses
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Demonstrating an awareness of risks associated with chemicals such as household chemicals: cleaning detergents, insect killers, medicines, perfume, garden and farm chemicals: fertilizer, pesticides, insect killers.

sub_topics.title: Care for Environment
sub_topics.introduction: This sub–topic will focus on equipping learners with skills, knowledge and attitude to develop the environment by picking litter and disposing off waste correctly.

teacher_steps: Explain to the learners what litter is.
Identify litter in the environment with learners
Explain to the learners on the different ways of disposing litter such as throwing in the bin or in the rubbish pit or burning so as to keep the environment clean.
learner_steps: Learn different ways of disposing litter correctly
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Developing care for the environment by picking litter, disposing of waste correctly

questions: Teacher to ask learners to make a presentation on the importance of caring for the environment

sub_topics.title: Weather and Climate Change
sub_topics.introduction: This sub-topic focuses on developing an awareness of weather and climatic occurrences and the effects of climate change.
competences: Demonstrate awareness effects of climate change

instructional_blocks.hook: What causes coldness, hotness and wetness?
teacher_steps: Ask learners what causes coldness, hotness and wetness.
Explain to the learners the weather occurrences such as cold, hot and wet when they take place within the particular time of the day.
Demonstrate the importance of weather occurrences to learners.
learner_steps: Learn about weather occurrences (cold, hot, wet)
Understand the importance of weather occurrences
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Developing awareness of weather occurrence such as cold, hot and wet.

teacher_steps: Explain the meaning of climate change. For example, when weather occurrences take place for a longer period, it becomes climate.
Explain to learners the causes of climate change such as; excessive cutting down of trees without replacing the planting, poor method of farming, burning fuels
Discuss with learners the effects of climate change such as; floods, drought, shortage of food and heat waves.
Guide the discussion and consolidate on the effects of climate change.
learner_steps: Understand floods, drought, food shortage, and heat waves
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Demonstrating awareness of effects of climate change such as: heat waves; floods…

questions: Teacher to ask learners to make a presentation on the importance of caring for the environment

sub_topics.summary: This topic focused on exploring natural resources, comparing features of the environment, demonstrating an awareness of risks associated with household chemicals, developing care for the environment, developing awareness of weather occurrences, demonstrating awareness of the effects of climate change. This topic will help the learners to acquire knowledge and skills to develop critical thinking, creativity and innovation as well as environmental sustainability.

topics.introduction: This topic will focus on demonstrating understanding of subtraction of whole numbers by: subtracting single digit whole numbers in intervals of 1 to 6, 1 to 9 using concrete and semi concrete objects and transit to abstract by using the subtraction symbol (take away, finding the difference, finding missing parts or finding less), carrying out activities involving zero to show that: 'when zero is subtracted from a number the result is that number, when a number is subtracted from itself the result is zero'. Subtracting double digit numbers up to 18 using the unit of 10. Subtracting whole numbers in 10s up to 100, relating subtraction to addition such as 6 – 2 = 4 or 4 + 2= 6, Completing subtraction of number sentences and using subtraction to carry out shopping activities involving money.

key_terms.definition: the space of time between two numbers
a digit is a single symbol or character used to represent a value in a numerical system e.g. 1,2,3,4,5…………..
How many of something in a set.
objects that are felt and seen
something that cannot be felt and be seen

sub_topics.title: Subtraction
sub_topics.introduction: This sub-topic will focus on demonstrating an understanding of subtraction of whole numbers.
competences: Demonstrate an understanding of subtraction of whole numbers
